windows.embed_text_v2: rolling and shaping white wax by hand into a tapered candle, peeling wrapper sheet on green cutting mat, forming wick tip, hand-rolled taper, white wax
windows.summary_v2: Hands on a green cutting mat unwrap a sheet of white wax and roll it by hand into a smooth white tapered candle.

tags_v2.action: rolling wax sheet into a tapered candle by hand
tags_v2.setting: green self-healing cutting mat on workbench

seconds_v2.desc: On a green cutting mat, two hands hold a folded white plastic/paper wrapper over a flattened sheet of white wax; a 'VIDEODUDE' watermark sits top-left.
Right hand peels back a corner of the white wrapper, exposing a soft white wax strip running down the middle of the sheet.
Hands fold a flap of the white sheet diagonally across, partially covering the white wax strip on the mat.
The wrapper is opened flat, revealing a thick rolled rope of white wax laid vertically down the center of the sheet.
Full white wax rope lies exposed on the opened sheet; a hand begins reaching in from the lower right.
Both hands move to the base of the white wax rope, starting to lift and shape it off the sheet.
Right hand grips and rolls the elongated white wax rope, smoothing it into a tapering candle form.
Hands continue rolling and compressing the white wax against the sheet, refining the taper shape.
Hands hold up the finished smooth white tapered candle horizontally, a small wick tip visible at one end, against the green mat.

windows.embed_text_v2: shaping and bending soft white spiral taper candles by hand into curled looped coils on a green cutting mat, molding pliable wax candles into letter shapes, no container
windows.summary_v2: A hand bends soft white twisted taper candles into curled, looped letter-like spiral shapes on a green cutting mat beside three finished tapers.

tags_v2.action: bending and coiling soft candles by hand

seconds_v2.desc: Three finished white spiral taper candles lie on a green cutting mat; a hand at right bends a soft fourth candle into a curl.
The hand continues coiling the pliable white candle, pinching its base into a tight round loop.
The fingers press the soft candle into a near-circular spiral, holding the looped wax upright.
The hand reshapes the curl, the bent white candle forming a hook-like loop in the fingers.
The molded loop is set down beside the three tapers as the hand steadies the coiled wax on the mat.
A finished spiral 'P'-shaped curl now rests on the mat next to the three straight twisted candles.
The hand picks up another soft candle at right and begins bending it into a second curled letter shape.
Fingers coil the new candle into a looped 'g'-like form beside the earlier curl.
The hand holds the second coiled candle in place, both curled wax shapes lined up with the three tapers.
